working_patterns: Replacing uniform-radius grid heuristics with full SLSQP optimization over (x,y,r) for all 26 circles jumped scores from ~0.78 to ~0.99
Providing analytic Jacobians for both the objective (-sum(r)) and pairwise non-overlap constraints speeds convergence and avoids numerical noise
Interleaved variable layout [x1,y1,r1,...] with per-pair closure-captured fun/jac yields clean, correct gradients
Multi-start SLSQP from varied seeds (grid, hex, perturbed) pushes score from 0.991 to 0.996
Applying a tiny post-optimization radius shrink (epsilon) to guarantee strict feasibility prevents the validity check from zeroing the score
Using hexagonal/staggered initial placements breaks symmetry better than pure rectangular grids
failing_patterns: Relying on a single seed (plain grid) caps the local optimum around 0.989-0.991
Using np.sqrt in constraints (instead of squared-distance form) adds numerical issues and needs 1e-30 padding
Long evaluation times (~3.2s) from many restarts leave little headroom for further multi-start expansion
Not applying a feasibility-shrink epsilon causes ~1e-15 slack violations that fail strict_pass
hypotheses: Circle packing has many local optima; SLSQP converges to a nearby local minimum, so seed diversity is the dominant driver of final score
Squared-distance non-overlap constraints (dx^2+dy^2-(ri+rj)^2) are smoother and cheaper than sqrt-based ones, improving SLSQP stability
Known Packomania optimum for n=26 is ~2.6356; current best 2.6256 suggests the search is stuck in a suboptimal combinatorial arrangement (wrong contact graph), not just needing more local refinement
Analytic Jacobians matter because finite-difference over 78 vars x 425 constraints is both slow and noisy near active constraints
suggested_directions: Add more diverse seeds: known near-optimal Packomania layout for n=26 as a hardcoded starting point, plus hex variants with (4,5,4,5,4,4) row patterns
Implement a basin-hopping-style perturbation loop: after each SLSQP solve, jitter the worst-packed circles (smallest radii) and re-optimize

Replace per-pair Python-closure constraints with a single vectorized constraint function returning all pair slacks plus a dense Jacobian to cut evaluation overhead and allow more restarts within time budget
Try a two-phase approach: first optimize with equal-radius constraint to find good combinatorial structure, then release radii for final SLSQP polish
Add a 'swap and re-optimize' move: swap positions of two circles with very different radii and re-run SLSQP to escape local optima
Increase maxiter and tighten ftol (e.g., 1e-13) on the best-so-far seed as a final refinement step
Explicitly apply radius shrink of ~1e-9 before returning to guarantee strict feasibility
